description: Geriatric weakness and decreased appetite without clear localization; defensible ESI 2 or 3.

patient.name: Dorothy W., 78F
patient.chief_complaint: General weakness, mild confusion baseline-changed, decreased appetite 2 days. Family says 'just not acting right'. Walking with assistance now whereas independent yesterday.
patient.history: Type 2 diabetes, hypertension, mild dementia at baseline, on metformin + lisinopril

rubric_notes: Geriatric 'not acting right' with baseline change = elevated risk floor. Differential includes occult sepsis (UTI/pneumonia), silent MI, stroke, hypoglycemia, dehydration. Temp 37.9 borderline fever, HR 96 just under tachycardia threshold, GCS 14 (baseline-changed mental status = critical per RUBRIC). Defensible ESI 2 (geriatric occult sepsis until ruled out) or ESI 3 (stable enough for general bed workup). The 'baseline-changed' mental status (GCS 14 from baseline 15) per RUBRIC §2 is concerning.
